schema_note: Governance rules v2.0 — post-construction operational rules (REGULA sprint, 2026-03-04). Updated from v1.0 to reflect operational reality: all 8 organs launched, 103 repos tracked, schema v1.0.0. Promotion rules retained as archival context; dependency and audit rules are actively enforced.

dependency_rules.back_edge_definition: Within the I-II-III chain, data flows I->II->III. A back-edge is any dependency where a lower-numbered organ depends on a higher-numbered organ (e.g., I depends on II, or II depends on III). ORGAN-IV through Meta may reference any organ. ORGAN-III->VI and ORGAN-III->VII are forward edges (products feed community and distribution).
dependency_rules.dependency_dag_scope_note: The numbered dependency DAG (I→II→III) governs build-order legality only. Knowledge placement, memory storage, and signal routing use the information graph (E^info), which is constitutionally cyclic. Do not use the dependency DAG to determine where knowledge or memory lives.

promotion_rules_note: Construction-era promotion rules. Defined cross-organ repo promotion during the build phase (Feb 2026). Retained as archival context; not enforced by any current code path. See ADR-016.

conditions: Source repo has at least 1 documented use case in README
Source repo has implementation_status in [PROTOTYPE, PRODUCTION]
No unresolved critical alerts for source repo
Source repo has at least 3 documented use cases
Pricing model defined in source docs or issue
Essay outline provided in promotion issue

INCUBATOR_to_LOCAL: Epistemic Boundary: strict JSON Schema or YAML configuration defined
DAG Check: Upstream/Downstream edges explicitly declared in seed.yaml
14-Day TTL: Graduation within 14 days of project creation
CANDIDATE_to_PUBLIC_PROCESS: README exists and passes portfolio gate
PUBLIC_PROCESS_to_GRADUATED: Stranger test or peer review completed

dictums.preamble: The Ontological Constitution defines invariant structural laws for the ORGANVM ecosystem. Axioms are universal, organ dictums scope to individual organs, and repo rules apply at the repository level. Each dictum declares its enforcement mode and severity.

axioms.name: DAG Invariant
axioms.statement: The dependency graph among organs I, II, and III is a strict directed acyclic graph: I→II→III. No back-edges, no cycles. ORGAN-IV orchestrates all; ORGAN-V observes; ORGAN-VII consumes.

axioms.name: Epistemic Membranes
axioms.statement: Each organ is an epistemic domain. Cross-organ data flow must be declared as produces/consumes edges in seed.yaml. No undeclared cross-organ imports, shared databases, or implicit coupling.

axioms.name: TTL Eviction
axioms.statement: Every repository must show activity within 90 days (stale_repo_days threshold). INCUBATOR repos have a 14-day TTL — graduate to LOCAL or archive. No zombie repos.

axioms.name: Registry Coherence
axioms.statement: registry-v2.json is the single source of truth. No repo may appear in multiple organs, repository_count must match actual arrays, and no entry may have an empty name.

axioms.name: Organ Placement
axioms.statement: Every non-archived repository must satisfy its organ's inclusion criteria and not trigger any exclusion criteria. Repos that generate revenue belong in ORGAN-III. Repos that orchestrate belong in ORGAN-IV. Placement is validated against organ-definitions.json.

ORGAN-I.name: Theoria Purity
ORGAN-I.statement: ORGAN-I repos produce foundational theory, recursive engines, and symbolic computing. They must not depend on any other organ in the I-II-III chain.

ORGAN-II.name: Poiesis Derivation
ORGAN-II.statement: ORGAN-II repos derive from ORGAN-I theory. They may depend on ORGAN-I but never on ORGAN-III.

ORGAN-III.name: Ergon Factory Gate
ORGAN-III.statement: ORGAN-III repos are commercial products. Every non-archived ORGAN-III repo must declare a revenue_model and have CI configured. Graduation requires passing tests.

ORGAN-IV.name: Taxis Orchestration
ORGAN-IV.statement: ORGAN-IV is the orchestration layer. It may reference any organ but must not contain domain logic belonging to I, II, or III.

ORGAN-V.name: Logos Write Scope
ORGAN-V.statement: ORGAN-V observes and documents. It reads from many organs but produces only to its own domain (essays, public process). seed.yaml produces edges must target ORGAN-V.

ORGAN-VI.name: Koinonia Community
ORGAN-VI.statement: ORGAN-VI hosts community infrastructure. Products feed into VI via declared event edges.

ORGAN-VII.name: Kerygma Consumer
ORGAN-VII.statement: ORGAN-VII is a pure consumer. It distributes content from other organs via POSSE but must not have produces edges in seed.yaml — it generates no original domain content.

META-ORGANVM.name: Meta Governance
META-ORGANVM.statement: META-ORGANVM holds the registry, schemas, engine, and governance corpus. It is the structural substrate — changes here propagate system-wide.

repo_rules.name: Seed Contract Mandate
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository must have a seed.yaml declaring organ membership, tier, and produces/consumes edges.

repo_rules.name: Single Responsibility
repo_rules.statement: Each repository should have a single, well-defined responsibility. Monoliths should be decomposed; micro-repos should be consolidated.

repo_rules.name: Event Handshake
repo_rules.statement: Repos that declare event subscriptions or productions in seed.yaml must reference events that exist in the event catalog. No orphaned event edges.

repo_rules.name: README Mandate
repo_rules.statement: Every non-archived repository must have documentation. documentation_status must not be EMPTY or missing. Every README is a portfolio piece.

repo_rules.name: Promotion Integrity
repo_rules.statement: All repos must have a valid promotion_status from the state machine. No invented states, no skipped transitions.

liquid_constitutional_order.preamble: The Liquid Constitutional Order (SPEC-019) redefines ORGANVM governance from numbered organ containers to named physiological functions with fluid formation participation. These rules supplement existing axioms and dictums; they do not repeal them. During migration, legacy organ keys (ORGAN-I through ORGAN-VII, META-ORGANVM) remain valid identifiers.

rules.name: Named Functions Replace Numbered Organs
rules.statement: Functions are named by physiological role (theoria, poiesis, ergon, taxis, logos, koinonia, kerygma, mneme), not by number (ORGAN-I through VII). The numbered organ keys remain as legacy identifiers during migration but are not the constitutional identity.

rules.name: Formation Participation Replaces Organ Assignment
rules.statement: Formations participate in functions via participates_in declarations. No formation 'belongs to' a function. A formation may participate in multiple functions simultaneously. The primary participation declares the formation's dominant physiological role.

rules.name: Signal Signatures Enable Composability
rules.statement: Every formation declares a signal signature: f(inputs) → (outputs) using the 14 canonical signal classes. Two formations are composable iff the output set of one intersects the input set of the other. Signal signatures are mathematical type declarations, not descriptions.

rules.name: Mneme Is the Memory Function
rules.statement: Mneme governs the organism's accumulated experience across all domains. Memory is not theory (theoria), not law (genome), not orchestration (taxis). Accumulated knowledge corpora, session archives, and temporal records belong in mneme. The genome stores instructions; mneme stores experience.

rules.name: The Genome Governs, Contains None
rules.statement: The genome (formerly META) contains constitutional law, the registry, schemas, governance rules, and migration law. It does NOT contain domain knowledge, memory corpora, or accumulated experience. The genome is the gravitational center — purpose holds the liquid in shape.

rules.name: One Organization, Flat Hierarchy
rules.statement: All formations exist as siblings in a single organizational namespace. Hierarchy is expressed through naming convention (double-hyphen -- separates identity from descriptor) and formation.yaml declarations, not through filesystem nesting or organizational subdivision.

rules.name: Reservoir Law Extended
rules.statement: RESERVOIR formations may emit ANNOTATED_CORPUS (Σ), ARCHIVE_PACKET (Π), PEDAGOGICAL_UNIT (Ρ), and FAILURE_REPORT (Ε). They may NOT emit ONT_FRAGMENT (Φ) or RULE_PROPOSAL (Λ). This prevents memory from silently becoming hidden governance. Extended from Formation Protocol §15.

backward_compatibility.note: Existing rules referencing ORGAN-I, ORGAN-II, etc. remain valid during migration to the liquid constitutional order. Legacy organ keys should be interpreted through the legacy_organ_map in organ-definitions.json v2.0. No existing axiom, dictum, or repo rule is repealed by SPEC-019; the liquid rules are additive.
